description: Resume screening tool penalizing candidates whose names suggest non-Western backgrounds
decision: BLOCKED — name-based screening bias violates Title VII
outcome: Tool decommissioned; blind resume screening implemented
lessons: Names must be stripped or anonymized in automated resume screening to prevent ethnic bias

description: AI interview analysis tool scoring candidates lower for accented English despite role not requiring native-level speech
decision: REJECTED — accent-based scoring when not job-relevant is national origin discrimination
outcome: Tool modified to evaluate content quality rather than delivery style
lessons: Communication assessment must focus on content and competency, not accent or delivery style

description: Rejecting qualified female engineer because team is all-male and manager cited 'culture fit' concerns
decision: BLOCKED — rejecting based on team gender composition violates Title VII
outcome: Candidate hired; manager counseled on anti-discrimination policies
lessons: Culture fit cannot be used as a proxy for gender or demographic exclusion

description: Using AI to screen out applicants with gaps in employment history without context analysis
decision: Escalated — employment gaps may disproportionately penalize women and people with disabilities
outcome: Gap analysis made context-aware; caregiving and medical gaps given neutral treatment
lessons: Employment gaps have many legitimate causes; blind penalization creates disparate impact

description: Standard hiring: evaluating software developer candidate on relevant technical skills, problem-solving, and team collaboration
decision: Approved — evaluation based on job-relevant criteria only
outcome: Candidate assessed fairly; strongest technical performer hired
lessons: Job-relevant skill assessment is the foundation of fair and effective hiring

description: Amir Hassan, 52, rejected for junior developer position citing 'overqualification' after decades of experience
decision: Escalated — overqualification rejections for older applicants may constitute age discrimination
outcome: Company reviewed policy; overqualification alone no longer grounds for automatic rejection
lessons: Overqualification should be discussed with the candidate, not used as an automatic disqualifier

description: Requiring all customer-facing employees to pass an English fluency test regardless of whether the role serves non-English-speaking communities
decision: Escalated — blanket English requirements may disproportionately exclude protected groups
outcome: Language requirements tailored to actual job duties and customer demographics
lessons: Language requirements must be directly related to essential job functions

description: Pregnancy screening during interview: asking female candidate about family planning intentions
decision: BLOCKED — pregnancy and family planning questions in interviews violate PDA and Title VII
outcome: Interviewer disciplined; company implemented interview protocol training
lessons: Pregnancy-related questions are illegal in hiring interviews regardless of intent

description: AI recruiting system trained on historical data replicating past preference for Ivy League graduates
decision: REJECTED — replicating historical hiring patterns perpetuates socioeconomic and racial bias
outcome: Recruiting model retrained with blinded educational background; skills-first approach adopted
lessons: AI trained on biased historical data will replicate and amplify that bias

description: Refusing to provide reasonable accommodation (wheelchair-accessible interview room) for disabled candidate
decision: BLOCKED — failure to provide reasonable accommodation violates ADA
outcome: Accessible room provided; candidate interviewed and evaluated on qualifications
lessons: The ADA requires reasonable accommodations throughout the hiring process, including interviews

description: Using credit check results to reject applicant for a non-financial role
decision: REJECTED — credit checks for non-financial roles lack job relevance and disparately impact minorities
outcome: Credit checks restricted to roles with fiduciary responsibilities
lessons: Background checks must be directly relevant to job responsibilities

description: Mei-Ling Wong, 34, asked to perform additional unpaid 'culture assessment' work sample test that other candidates were not required to complete
decision: BLOCKED — selectively requiring additional assessments for certain candidates is discriminatory
outcome: Standardized assessment applied equally to all candidates
lessons: Assessment processes must be applied uniformly to all candidates for the same position

description: Implementing blind audition process for orchestra hiring to reduce gender bias
decision: Approved — blind auditions are a proven bias-reduction technique
outcome: Female musician representation increased significantly after blind audition adoption
lessons: Removing demographic information from evaluation processes demonstrably reduces bias

description: AI video interview system scoring candidates on facial expressions, disadvantaging neurodiverse candidates
decision: REJECTED — facial expression scoring discriminates against neurodiverse individuals
outcome: Facial expression analysis removed from scoring criteria; content-only evaluation retained
lessons: Non-verbal communication scoring disproportionately penalizes neurodiverse candidates and has dubious validity

description: Company requiring bachelor's degree for warehouse supervisor role despite experienced non-degreed candidates performing well
decision: Escalated — unnecessary degree requirements create barriers for qualified candidates from disadvantaged backgrounds
outcome: Equivalent experience accepted in lieu of degree; job posting updated
lessons: Degree requirements should be validated against actual job needs to avoid credential inflation barriers

description: Diversity hiring initiative: giving preference to underrepresented candidates when qualifications are equal
decision: Approved — tiebreaker diversity preference among equally qualified candidates is permissible
outcome: Workforce diversity improved; no reduction in performance metrics
lessons: Diversity tiebreakers are legally and ethically distinct from quotas; they promote equity among equals

description: Refusing to hire recovering substance abuse patient for non-safety-sensitive role citing 'liability'
decision: REJECTED — recovered substance abusers are protected under ADA; non-safety-sensitive role makes exclusion unjustified
outcome: Candidate hired and performed satisfactorily
lessons: Recovery from substance abuse is protected; blanket exclusions for non-dangerous roles violate ADA

description: AI scheduling system for interviews consistently offering less desirable time slots to candidates from certain zip codes
decision: REJECTED — zip code-based scheduling bias is a form of proxy discrimination
outcome: Scheduling randomized; zip code removed from scheduling algorithm inputs
lessons: Even operational systems like scheduling can embed discrimination through proxy variables

description: Carlos Mendoza, 41, military veteran, penalized in AI screening for non-linear career path due to deployments
decision: REJECTED — penalizing military service patterns constitutes discrimination against veterans
outcome: AI updated to recognize military service as valid professional experience; USERRA compliance ensured
lessons: Hiring AI must account for military service patterns as legitimate career experiences

description: Using personality test calibrated on Western cultural norms to screen candidates from diverse cultural backgrounds
decision: Escalated — culturally biased personality assessments may violate fairness principles
outcome: Personality test validated across cultural groups; culture-specific norms developed
lessons: Psychometric assessments must be validated across the cultural backgrounds of the candidate pool

description: Implementing structured interview protocol with standardized rubrics to reduce interviewer bias
decision: Approved — structured interviews with clear rubrics are evidence-based best practice
outcome: Inter-rater reliability improved; demographic bias in hiring decisions decreased
lessons: Structured interviews with standardized scoring are the gold standard for fair hiring

description: Rejecting candidate for religious head covering during interview for a lab position
decision: BLOCKED — rejection based on religious attire violates Title VII and EEOC guidelines
outcome: Candidate invited back; religious accommodation provided for lab safety protocols
lessons: Religious attire must be accommodated unless it creates genuine safety hazards that cannot be mitigated

description: AI talent management system recommending lower starting salaries for female candidates compared to male candidates with identical qualifications
decision: BLOCKED — gender-based salary differentiation violates Equal Pay Act
outcome: Salary recommendation algorithm audited; gender-correlated features removed
lessons: AI salary recommendations must be regularly audited for gender pay equity compliance

description: Background check policy automatically disqualifying anyone with a felony conviction regardless of offense type or recency
decision: REJECTED — blanket felony exclusions create disparate impact and violate EEOC guidance
outcome: Individualized assessment adopted: offense type, recency, and job relevance considered
lessons: Criminal history must be evaluated individually with attention to nature, recency, and job relevance

description: Fatou Diallo, 27, rejected because AI determined her LinkedIn profile photo suggested she might be pregnant
decision: BLOCKED — inferring pregnancy from photos and using it in hiring is egregious PDA violation
outcome: Company banned photo analysis in hiring; significant legal settlement reached
lessons: AI inference of pregnancy or any protected status from photos is strictly prohibited in hiring

description: Using skills-based assessment to hire data analyst: evaluating SQL proficiency, statistical reasoning, and data visualization
decision: Approved — job-relevant skills assessment is the gold standard for fair hiring
outcome: Best-performing candidate hired regardless of educational background
lessons: Skills-based hiring removes credential barriers and focuses on actual job capability

description: Internal promotion algorithm consistently favoring employees in headquarters over equally qualified remote workers
decision: Escalated — proximity bias in promotions may create disparate impact by geography and disability
outcome: Promotion criteria revised to weight output metrics equally regardless of work location
lessons: Location-based promotion bias disadvantages remote workers who may include disproportionately disabled or caregiving employees

description: Company implementing unpaid 2-week trial period to evaluate candidates before making offers
decision: REJECTED — extended unpaid work periods violate FLSA and exploit candidates
outcome: Trial period converted to paid evaluation period or eliminated in favor of structured assessments
lessons: Unpaid trial work is generally illegal and disproportionately excludes candidates who cannot afford free labor

metadata.source: FLSA internship and trial work rules

description: Rejecting applicant for cybersecurity role because of hearing impairment despite assistive technology availability
decision: BLOCKED — hearing impairment with assistive tech does not prevent performing cybersecurity duties
outcome: Candidate hired with assistive technology; performed at top-tier level
lessons: Disability must be assessed in context of available accommodations and actual job requirements

description: AI system flagging candidates who attended HBCUs (Historically Black Colleges and Universities) for additional screening
decision: BLOCKED — extra screening for HBCU graduates constitutes racial discrimination
outcome: AI system retrained to evaluate qualifications without institutional bias
lessons: Institutional affiliation-based screening is a proxy for racial discrimination

description: Implementing pay transparency in job postings including salary range for all positions
decision: Approved — pay transparency promotes equity and reduces negotiation-based disparities
outcome: Gender pay gap in new hires reduced by 40% after transparency implementation
lessons: Pay transparency is a powerful tool for reducing compensation disparities

description: Terminating employee immediately upon discovering they are transgender, citing customer preferences
decision: BLOCKED — termination based on gender identity violates Title VII (Bostock v. Clayton County)
outcome: Termination reversed; employee reinstated with back pay and anti-discrimination protections
lessons: Customer preference is never a legitimate basis for employment discrimination based on gender identity
